# 'gs branch submit' should not auto-upgrade to --force-with-lease
# in merge restack mode.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-04-17T09:00:00Z'

env GIT_SPICE_VERBOSE=true

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub register alice
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# merge-based restacking is the default; set explicitly for clarity
git config spice.restack.method merge

# create and submit a branch
git add feature.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature' feature
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

# create a non-fast-forward scenario in another clone
cd $WORK
shamhub clone alice/example fork
cd fork
git checkout feature
cp $WORK/extra/remote-update.txt feature.txt
git add feature.txt
git commit -m 'Remote update'
git push

# local branch diverges; submit should not use force-with-lease
cd $WORK/repo
git fetch origin feature
cp $WORK/extra/local-update.txt feature.txt
git add feature.txt
git commit -m 'Local update'

! gs branch submit
stderr 'non-fast-forward'
stderr 'run again with --force'
! stderr 'lease\.ref=feature'

gs branch submit --force
stderr 'Updated #'

-- repo/feature.txt --
base

-- extra/remote-update.txt --
base
remote update

-- extra/local-update.txt --
base
local update
